blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It's estimated that Master went to heaven around 80 years ago. Do you know what that means?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I don't.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The rank assigned to Master by the Magic Association was different before he went to heaven and after he returned. In other words, he became an Archmage after realizing something in heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Every mage dreams of becoming an Archmage. Knowing this, it doesn't make sense not to go to heaven.
Shirone had to agree.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: If Arcane had become a third-class mage after returning from heaven, it meant there was something in heaven that had awakened him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Seeing Shirone waver, Canis revealed the last piece of information he had.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Most of the notes left by Master were just words, but at the end, there was a single sentence.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What did it say?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It was something like this: 'That place is the origin of everything. The source of Schema and...'
Rian's eyes lit up.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Schema?
Ignoring him, Canis continued.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Forgotten ancient magic…

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ancient magic?
Tess shouted.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Ancient weapons!
Canis frowned.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: ...Are you doing this on purpose?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Hohoho! Sorry, please continue.
Annoyed by Tess's jest, Canis eventually recited the entire sentence.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It was written like this: 'I leave this at Metagate.'
It was a note left to prevent those unaware of heaven's existence from accessing the information.
Even Harvest, who inherited Arcane's memories, had wandered the continent to find the secret location.
The fact that he had gone to such lengths to obscure it showed how much he didn't want this information to be known.
Nevertheless, he left a record... perhaps it was an inescapable mischievous streak.
Though it was just a single sentence, the note contained crucial information.
First, heaven had Schema and magic.
Therefore, it was likely inhabited by humans.
Additionally, ancient relics would likely be piled high there.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arcane was saying through the note: 'Examiners and mages of the world, beyond the Metagate lie mountains of ancient relics.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, what were these ancient relics? No history book mentioned them.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: A prehistorical world awaited them.
The origin of humanity, which no scholar had unveiled, might be hidden somewhere in the immeasurable sky.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I've told you everything I know.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The conclusion is, we will go there.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: To honor Master's will and to further our magic.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin and I have agreed on this.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Whatever happens, there will be no resentment.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I-I want to go too!

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: If Amy's going, then so am I! Shirone, let's go!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. I'm not taking anyone. I'm not going either.
Everyone was dumbfounded by Shirone's words.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was too precious to give up just like that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This was an opportunity that no mage in the world would ever have.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Didn't Arcane also reach the rank of Archmage after gaining enlightenment in heaven?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I know it's dangerous. But doesn't Canis have a way back?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: That's not the point.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Whether there's an exit or not doesn't matter.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Just because I can open the gate to heaven doesn't mean I should put you all in danger.
Canis gritted his teeth.
He had known Shirone's philanthropic spirit was like a cliff you couldn't cross.
That's why he had crafted a meticulous plan to bring them to this point.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If you're that scared, then don't go. But we had a deal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You have to let Arin and me through heaven's gate.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: No. I won't let you go either.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You bastard! Are you betraying us now?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Think whatever you want.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But now you're a fellow student at our school.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can't open the gate and send you to your death.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who asked you to worry about my life?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'll handle my own affairs!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: And we can always come back!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Put yourself in my shoes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If you were me, could you ask us to go through heaven's gate?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could you ask us to go to the star we just saw?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: 'No, not even if I died.'
That was the issue.
It was a matter separate from the risk.
Lost in thought, Amy flicked Shirone on the forehead.
Shirone, holding his stinging forehead, looked at her in disbelief as Amy spoke.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Of course, I'd say let's go to heaven.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: This is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.
Amy was lying.
Worrying about a friend's safety also hinders clear judgment.
She thought it best to lessen Shirone's burden first.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't lie.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is really dangerous.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even with the Metagate, do you know where it leads?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We're not going to come right back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The more determined we are to go, the greater the risk.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why don't you understand that?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Then why did you bring me here?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? That's…

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You said you'd feel reassured with me.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Isn't that why you came to my house?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Or what, were you planning to go sightseeing with a girl?
Amy aggressively pressed.
Shirone was speechless.
He had a responsibility for bringing a senior student here, wasting valuable time.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: I feel the same.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Even if you open the gate, it's my choice to follow or not.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You don't need to worry about that.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Besides, if we're going, Rian and I would be a great help.
In any party, a swordsman was crucial.
They had the bravery and animal instincts that mages lacked.
A second they created could be a life-or-death moment for a mage.
As Shirone hesitated, someone knocked on the door.
Canis hid the Metagate.
The knocker waited for permission before entering.
When Shirone allowed entry, Mahatu walked in.
An unwelcome guest.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: I apologize for earlier.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine, I've forgotten about it.
Of course, Amy hadn't.
But she let it slide, suspecting Mahatu had another reason for his visit.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Are you going to heaven?
Shirone didn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Please don't go.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What are you talking about? Don't go?
Mahatu crossed his arms.
As he inhaled, his muscles swelled, and a powerful aura emanated from his eyes.
It was an overwhelming presence.
Shirone's group felt as if they were being pushed back.
Maybe they were.
If he had added killing intent, they wouldn't have been able to breathe.
Not intending to offend, Mahatu quickly withdrew his presence.
But the air was still heavy.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: I apologize for overstepping.
No one thought Mahatu's apology was sincere.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: I cannot go to heaven.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: I failed to prove my strength in the Room of Accomplishment and Sacrifice.
Shirone understood Mahatu's intent.
He was indicating the difficulty of heaven based on his own strength.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Do Kergosians also take the test?

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Of course. Proving one's strength in the Room of Accomplishment and Sacrifice grants a Kergosian warrior the right to go to heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But to go there…

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Yes. An Unlocker is necessary.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: When the gate to heaven buried in volcanic ash was restored, many went to heaven.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: But few returned.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Therefore, Miro established the Room of Accomplishment and Sacrifice to verify strength.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Though Unlockers are an exception, it's best not to go unless you can pass the test.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The fact that few had returned was surprising, but it was even more shocking that Mahatu mentioned Miro's name.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How do you know Miro? Did you meet her?

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: No, but most leaders know of her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can you tell us more? We came here to learn about Miro.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: Indeed, I see.
Mahatu understood why Shirone hadn't been swayed by gold or women.
Someone connected to Miro was certainly different from the usual indulgent Unlockers.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: 500 years ago, the Kergos civil war broke out.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: It was a conflict between those who followed the angels and those who opposed them.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: The anti-angel faction won.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: But a sudden volcanic eruption nearly wiped out the tribe.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: The angel followers saw the eruption as a disaster sent by the angels.
Scholars were wrong to think Lupe started the civil war.
The involvement of angels made it a top-secret known only to Kergo's high-ranking officials.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: 250 years later, an outsider named McClain Goffin visited.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: He was an Unlocker like you, Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: He restored the Kergo ruins buried in volcanic ash and opened the gate to heaven.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: We call that gate Goffin's Gate.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: But it caused another turmoil.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: The conflicts from 250 years ago reemerged.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: This time, the angel followers won, changing the regime.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: That's the current Kergos.
